task_completion.description: Verify the agent is logged in as an administrator.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The top right user profile menu or header indicates the user is logged in as 'Admin' or 'Administrator'.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent navigated to the 'World History' (HIST201) course.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The course title 'World History' or shortname 'HIST201' is visible in the page header, breadcrumbs, or navigation menu.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent accessed the Notes section for student David Lee.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The screen shows the 'Notes' page or 'Add a new note' form specifically for the user 'David Lee' (or username 'dlee').

task_completion.description: Verify the correct disciplinary note content was entered.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text area contains the text mentioning the student kept talking during the guest lecture and was assigned detention (keywords: talking, guest lecture, detention).

task_completion.description: Verify the note context/publish state is set to 'Course'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Context', 'Publish to', or 'Status' dropdown menu for the note is set to 'Course'.

task_completion.description: Verify the note was successfully saved and is visible in the system.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A confirmation message appears, or the saved note is displayed under 'Course notes' on David Lee's notes page.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used a web browser to interact with Moodle.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show a standard web browser interface (like Chromium/Firefox) with the Moodle application loaded, rather than terminal commands or database clients.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually typed and submitted the form in the Moodle UI.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text is entered into the actual Moodle text editor/textarea, and dropdowns are interacted with, rather than text being pasted into a generic text editor.

integrity.description: Verify the final success screen is a genuine Moodle page render.
integrity.visual_evidence: The page layout, fonts, and Moodle UI elements appear consistent and untampered, with no browser developer tools open to fake the success state.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target Student: David Lee (username: dlee). Target Course: 'World History' (HIST201). Note content must include keywords: 'talking', 'guest lecture', and 'detention'. The publish state/context must be set to 'course' level.
